nameMeanings.name: Kristina
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From a feminine form of Latin Christianus ( see Christina ). Queen Kristina of Sweden ( 1626–89 ) succeeded her father Gustavus Adolphus in 1632 . Growing to adulthood, she presided over a glittering court, but in 1654 she abdicated, left Sweden dressed as a man, converted to Roman Catholicism, and lived for most of the rest of her life in Rome.

variants.name: Christina
meanings: Simplified form of Latin Christiana, feminine form of Christianus ( see Christian ), or a Latinized form of Middle English Christin ‘Christian’ (Old English christen, from Latin).

historicEvents.title: Christianity Becomes Official Religion of Sweden
historicEvents.description: King Olof Skotkonung converts to Christianity and makes it the official religion of Sweden. Skotkonungj was baptized by the missionary Sigrid in Husaby in Vastergotland. Skotkonung was the first Swedish king to remain Christian throughout his reign.

historicEvents.title: Vreta Abbey is First Christian Convent
historicEvents.description: Vreta Abbey was established around 1100 and was the first Christian convent in Sweden. It was located in modern-day Linkoping Municipality. It was founded on orders of Pope Paschal II with large donations by King Inge the Elder and Queen Helena.

en.lifeSummary: Kristina Ingesdotter was born in Sweden as the daughter of Inge Stenkilsson and Helena, Queen of Sweden. She had at least 8 sons and 10 daughters with Mstislav I Vladimirovich Grand Prince of Kiev. She died on 18 January 1122, in Kyiv, Ukraine.
